source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition reflecting the CONRAT/UPV pod bogie layout called out in the original brief: 4130 welded 30x30x3 box-tube chassis beams, cross frames, 4 bogie skate plates, levitation module mount, aeroshell attachment, and payload compartment. Part families correspond to the physical weld/bolt groups of the bogie subassembly. No rulebook citations fabricated.

full_prompt:
Design the levitation-bogie mount frame of a student hyperloop-class pod prototype inspired by the Hyperloop research university / HyperPoland variant, intended for integration with a 125 m test track at reduced pressure (100 Pa). The pod sits on two bogies (front + rear), each carrying two passive permanent-magnet Halbach-array levitation skates plus one guidance rail-roller. This design scope is limited to one bogie frame: length 700 mm, width 500 mm, height 200 mm. Each bogie supports 50% of total pod wet mass (pod 140 kg wet), so 70 kg static per bogie + dynamic + emergency-brake loads. The bogie frame is a welded 30 x 30 x 3 mm box-tube frame plus 4 mm plate mounts for the skate magnet arrays, made of AISI 4130 chromoly (E 205 GPa, yield 460 MPa, ultimate 560 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). The permanent-magnet levitation force is proportional to the gap between track and magnet; nominal gap is 10 mm producing 700 N per skate lift, and the gap is stabilized by a rubber damped mount between skate and bogie frame. Load cases: (LC1) static trim at zero speed: 70 kg x 9.81 = 687 N distributed across two skates + one guide roller; (LC2) cruise 100 km/h nominal: lift 2 x 700 N = 1400 N vertical per bogie; aero drag applied at bogie-frame lower face 120 N horizontal; (LC3) emergency brake: 2 g longitudinal deceleration on 70 kg per bogie = 1373 N horizontal fore-aft at guide-roller mount; (LC4) curved-section cornering: 0.5 g lateral on 70 kg = 343 N at guide-roller; (LC5) hard-landing bogie-track gap closure: gap drops to 5 mm transiently, skate force triples to 2100 N per skate = 4200 N vertical per bogie; (LC6) safety factor: apply FoS 2.0 over all cases per European Hyperloop Week conservative gate. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC6 factored loads, max von Mises must remain below 460 MPa yield; (R2) bogie frame dry mass must not exceed 12 kg (one bogie); (R3) first natural frequency of bogie (with magnet mass, approx 8 kg per skate x 2 = 16 kg plus roller 3 kg = 19 kg loaded) must exceed 45 Hz to clear magnet-array levitation oscillation band (typical 5-15 Hz); (R4) peak vertical deflection at skate attachment under LC5 must remain below 1 mm to preserve levitation gap integrity; (R5) skate-mounting bolt preload loss under LC2 + LC3 combined must remain below 10%. Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 bogie frame assembly, magnet-array interface dims, FEA per load case, modal report with lumped masses, mass budget.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the pod bogie physical build: two longitudinal pod chassis beams welded to three cross frames, four bogie plates (one per Halbach-array skate), a levitation module mount carrying the magnet assemblies, an aeroshell attachment bracket joining bogie to pod aeroshell, and a payload compartment bolted into the pod body above the bogie. Interfaces are MIG-welded for beams/cross frames/skate plates, bolted for levitation module/aeroshell/payload, with rubber-damped isolators between skate plate and levitation module. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC6 factored LC5 gap closure). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the levitation-bogie mount frame of a student hyperloop-class pod prototype inspired by the Hyperloop research university / HyperPoland variant, intended for integration with a 125 m test track at reduced pressure (100 Pa).

Envelope: one bogie frame, length 700 mm, width 500 mm, height 200 mm. Pod 140 kg wet; each bogie supports 50% (70 kg static per bogie). Welded 30 x 30 x 3 mm box-tube frame plus 4 mm plate mounts for magnet arrays. Nominal levitation gap 10 mm produces 700 N per skate lift.

Material: AISI 4130 chromoly (yield 460 MPa, ultimate 560 MPa).

Load cases: (LC1) static trim: 70 kg x 9.81 = 687 N across two skates + guide roller; (LC2) cruise 100 km/h nominal: 2 x 700 N = 1400 N vertical per bogie + 120 N aero drag horizontal; (LC3) emergency brake 2 g on 70 kg = 1373 N fore-aft at guide-roller mount; (LC4) cornering 0.5 g lateral on 70 kg = 343 N at guide-roller; (LC5) hard-landing gap closure to 5 mm, skate force triples to 2100 N per skate = 4200 N vertical per bogie; (LC6) FoS 2.0 over all cases per European Hyperloop Week conservative gate.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC6 factored max vM < 460 MPa yield; (R2) bogie frame dry mass <= 12 kg; (R3) first natural frequency (with 19 kg lumped) > 45 Hz; (R4) LC5 peak vertical deflection at skate attachment < 1 mm; (R5) LC2+LC3 skate-bolt preload loss < 10%.

prompt.title: CONRAT / Hyperloop UPV Student Pod Bogie Frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 4130 welded bogie frame 700x500x200 mm for passive-Halbach levitation, 70 kg per bogie, FoS 2.0 per EHW gate. Decomposition: 2x pod chassis beams, 3x cross frames, 4x bogie skate plates, levitation module mount, aeroshell attachment, payload compartment. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: pod_chassis_beam
parts.role: longitudinal 4130 box-tube beam pair forming the primary load path of the bogie

parts.name: cross_frame
parts.role: transverse 4130 box-tube cross frames welded between chassis beams to provide torsional stiffness

parts.name: bogie_skate_plate
parts.role: 4 mm plate mounts (one per Halbach skate) that carry levitation magnet arrays + 700 N nominal / 2100 N transient lift

parts.name: levitation_module_mount
parts.role: rubber-damped mount sub-bracket tying each skate plate to its magnet module and providing gap-stiffness isolation

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 4x M6 per module into skate plate

parts.name: aeroshell_attachment
parts.role: bracket bolted between the pod chassis beams and the pod aeroshell; reacts aero drag 120 N at bogie-frame lower face

parts.name: payload_compartment
parts.role: upper compartment bolted onto the chassis beams above bogie carrying non-structural payload mass

parts.name: guide_roller_mount
parts.role: single guide-roller mount at center-fore of bogie reacting LC3 emergency-brake 1373 N and LC4 cornering 343 N

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: bogie axes: X longitudinal (front+), Y lateral, Z vertical; origin at front-center of chassis beam pair

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield, with FoS 2.0 already in LC6

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (welded T-joints at beams/cross-frames, welded skate plates, bolted levitation module mount, bolted aeroshell/payload/guide-roller) <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC6 factored).
Skate-plate-to-cross-frame weld throat stress under LC5 must remain below 230 MPa (0.5 x yield) to keep welds elastic during gap-closure events.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for tubing + shell for plate + spring/bushing elements for rubber isolators
analysis_types: modal with lumped magnet + roller masses

interfaces.mediated_by: MIG-welded T-joints between longitudinal chassis beams and transverse cross frames
interfaces.load_path: torsion/bending closure for bogie; primary load path under LC1/LC5

interfaces.mediated_by: MIG-welded skate plates onto cross frames at each skate station (4 locations)
interfaces.load_path: Halbach lift load (700 N nominal, 2100 N transient) into frame

interfaces.mediated_by: rubber-damped bolted levitation module mount (4x M6 per module with elastomer isolator stack)
interfaces.load_path: levitation magnet inertial + gap-stiffness load path (R5 preload loss gate)

interfaces.mediated_by: 8x M8 bolted aeroshell attachment bracket onto chassis beams
interfaces.load_path: aero drag 120 N and aeroshell inertial loads into chassis

interfaces.mediated_by: 6x M6 bolted payload compartment onto chassis beams
interfaces.load_path: up-to-30-kg payload inertia under LC3 emergency brake transferred into beams

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M8 bolted guide-roller mount onto front cross frame
interfaces.load_path: LC3 (1373 N) and LC4 (343 N) guide-roller reactions into bogie

fem_check_policy.rationale: Skate-attachment deflection (R4) requires full welded frame plus plates; modal gate (R3) includes lumped magnet + roller masses that only live on the assembled model; bolt preload loss (R5) depends on relative stiffness of the assembly around the bolted joints.

notes.why_multi_part: The brief explicitly describes a welded box-tube frame plus plate skate mounts, rubber-damped magnet modules, a guide-roller mount, and integration with pod aeroshell + payload - an inherently multi-part assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts of cross frames and mount families reflect typical CONRAT/UPV-style bogie practice; no new rulebook thresholds invented.
